title: Help Wanted!
author: Magic-Mike-2023
selftext:
Hey guys!  
I'm looking for **10-15** people who has experience in building thematic portfolios.   
My team developed and launched an Ai-powered(Not LLM) stock selection and portfolio construction tool earlier this year called [Alpha Builder](https://builder.limex.com/). Some of you may seen it already if you joined our workshop in November. 

The basic is that you can define your style and investable universe, and the machine will figure out the best buys and sells defines by fundamentals, technicals, and macro trends. You would have full access, and could develop as many strategies as you want - the only thing we ask in return is feedback!!   
If you interested, please leave a comment or DM me with 1-2 sentence about your trading/investing experience and I will send a link to my Calendly to schedule a quick 1-1 call.

comments.author: YOLO401Kto0
comments.body:
Hey! the platform actually looks really good and the idea behind it is nice. I like the whole thematic portfolio approach and how the system builds everything for you like that’s genuinely useful.

But here’s the thing… WHY would I use your platform instead of just using something like ChatGPT or Gemini? Those already let me screen stocks, build strategies, analyze fundamentals and macros, and do a ton of other things for like ten bucks a month. I’ve been looking through your site for about an hour now trying to understand what the real advantage is and I still don’t see the clear “this is why we exist” reason.

If the big selling point is that it’s not an LLM and it’s built differently, cool, but what does that actually mean for me in terms of performance or outcomes? What am I getting here that I can’t get from the AI tools everyone already has in their pocket?

I’m asking this more from the investor side of my brain, I like the concept a lot and it definitely has potential, but before I hop in i just want to understand the real edge that makes this platform worth choosing over the cheaper and more flexible AI options out there?

replies.author: Magic-Mike-2023
replies.body:
Hey, happy to answer:)
The main difference between LLM models and Alpla Builder models is that there are different types of algos that are used for training. Under the hood of AB are mathematical and statistical ML algorithms that work better with numbers and allow you to get better predictions. They are not perfect and not always 100% correct, but in trading, even 51% correct predictions is a measure of success. Plus, they were used in the financial world way before chatGPT became popular.
So, when you create a trading strategy in AB, it takes your settings as an input and starts to look for drivers of the stocks that match those settings. As a result, you are getting a ranked list of stocks that updates weekly. The portfolio that is provided to you depends on how many stocks wanted to be in it and the goal. Some portfolios may underperform the market because they made for cash flow, low risk etc. (or settings are poorly combined) . Or you can build a strategy that outperforms SPY, and it may be more riskier. 

At the end of the day, you save yourself a lot of time on doing analysis and calculations and work with stocks that already match your investment goals. If you don't like the backtest results, you can always edit the settings of your strategy to improve it (or make them worse) 😉
